demos.question: What is the effect of changing stride of the convolution?
demos.gold_answer: By reducing the stride of the convolution with the hole algorithm, the authors were able to improve mAP by 26 points [1].
demos.answer: Subclaim 1: Reducing the stride of the convolution with the hole algorithm improves mAP by 2.6 points.

demos.question: Why author did 7.3 Non-english and computer languages tast? What is the objective of this section?
demos.gold_answer: They did Non-english and computer languages test to shows that the benefits of from pretraining have little to do with the format of the tasks [3]. Therefore, objective of this section is to show that advantage of pretraining persist with various degrees [1].
demos.answer:
Subclaim 1: The authors performed non-English and computer languages tests.
Subclaim 2: Non-English and computer languages tests show that the benefits from pretraining tasks are not derived from the format of the tasks.
Subclaim 3: The objective of this section is to show that advantage of pretraining persist with various degrees.

demos.question: How many different types of experiments are performed to test the proposed models?
demos.gold_answer: 5 different types of experiments are performed to test the proposed models [2]. They are Generalization over time scales, Experiments on MNIST, Experiments on Natural Image Patches, Out-of-domain Inputs, and Visualizing Features [3].
demos.answer:
Subclaim 1: 5 different types of experiments are performed to test the proposed models.
Subclaim 2: Generalization over time scales, Experiments on MNIST, Experiments on Natural Image Patches, Out-of-domain Inputs, and Visualizing Features are the different types of experiments performed to test the proposed models.

demos.question: Can the number of frames be extended to more than 16? If exists, what is the upper bound for the number of frames?
demos.gold_answer: They train a new masked frame interpolation and extrapolation network ↑F , capable of increasing the number of frames of the generated video either by frame interpolation for a smoother generated video, or by pre/post frame extrapolation for extending the video length [2]. Additionally, the spatial super-resolution models enable to increase a higher (controllable) frame rate [3]. Therefore, using the extrapolation network ↑F, it can possible to extend the video length from 16 frames to 76 frames [3].
demos.answer:
Subclaim 1: A new masked frame interpolation and extrapolation network ↑F can increase the number of frames of the generated video either by frame interpolation for a smoother generated video, or by pre/post frame extrapolation for extending the video length.
Subclaim 2: The spatial super-resolution models also enable an increase in frame rate.
Subclaim 3: The extrapolation network ↑F can extend the video length from 16 frames to 76 frames.

instructions: Instruction: Read the question and the gold answer. Write 1-3 concise subclaims directly supported by the gold answer only. Use lines starting with Subclaim 1:, Subclaim 2:, …
